# `trimext` Function

Remove the file extension from a path

Returns the path with its last extension removed. If the path has no extension it is returned unchanged.

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
trimext(path string) string
```

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `path` (String) The file path or name to strip the extension from.
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->


## Example Usage

### Basic usage

```console
> provider::util::trimext("config.yaml")
"config"

> provider::util::trimext("config.tar.gz")
"config.tar"

> provider::util::trimext("config")
"config"

> provider::util::trimext("path/to/config.yaml")
"path/to/config"
```

Only the last extension is removed. Hidden files (starting with `.`) with no other extension are returned unchanged.

### Loading YAML config files into a map

A common pattern is to combine `trimext` with `fileset` and `yamldecode` to load a directory of YAML files into a map keyed by config name. The glob pattern `*.{yaml,yml}` picks up both extensions in a single `fileset` call.

```terraform
locals {
  yaml_files = fileset(path.module, "config/*.{yaml,yml}")
  configs = {
    for f in local.yaml_files :
    provider::util::trimext(basename(f)) => yamldecode(file("${path.module}/${f}"))
  }
}

output "configs" {
  value = local.configs
}
# Result (given config/app.yaml, config/database.yml):
# {
#   "app"      = { port = 8080, replicas = 2 }
#   "database" = { host = "db.internal", port = 5432 }
# }
```
